Fig. 1: Schematic of the DLE combustion system layout for various engine ratings

*   a- Typhoon Engine
    *   Components: Engine Casing, Prechamber, Dilution, TBC, Impingement Cooling Liner, Transition Duct, Radial Inflow, Pilot Burner
*   b- Tornado Engine
     *   Components: DLE Burner, Pressure Casing, Combustor, Adapter, Transition Duct, Top Hat, Backplate, Diffuser
*   c- Tempest Engine
    *   Components: Pilot Burner Assembly, Engine casing, Radial Swirler, Combustion Chamber, Transition Duct
*   d- Cyclone Engine
    *   Components: Engine Case, Impingement Liner, Pilot Burner, Radial Swirler, Combustion Chamber, Transition Duct

Fig. 2: Cutaway of the Typhoon combustion system assembly

*   Components: Engine Casing, Pilot Burner Assembly, Swirler Burner Assembly, Skin,  Combustion Chamber + TBC

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   PRZ - primary reaction zone equivalence ratio
*   TET - turbine entry temperature
* Tempest is a 25% scale up of the Typhoon
* Tempest has six reverse flow tubular combustors connected by a transition duct.
* Cyclone has six reverse-flow tubular combustion chambers.

Here's the breakdown of the technical content extracted from the document:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Ageometry effective area = Σ Ai
*   Σ (Aci / Ageometry effective area) + Aleakages = 100%

**2. Graphs/Charts:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 3: Schematic simplification of ABB ALSTOM POWER combustion system test logic**
    *   Components with labels
        *   Combustion System Tests
        *   Air Flow Calibration Rig
        *   Atmospheric Rig
        *   High Pressure Rig Air Facilities
        *   Engine Test
        *   Full 1D-Combustion Aerothermal & Heat Transfer Design Analyses
        *   Minor Design Modification

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Engine efficiency exceeding 35%
*   Cyclone engine is 13.4 MW (18000 hp) type
*   Primary zone Mach number should not be >0.047
*   Orifice discharge coefficient to within ±2%

title: design_methods_of_the_Alstom_DLE_combustors
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 8]
Here's the breakdown of the technical content:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Msystem total = Sum( (Ae/A geometry effective area)* Mtotal air)
*   (ΔPcombustor/P1) = (R/2)*((Mair sqrt(T2))/(P2 Ae))^2 + (ΔP/Pt) heat addition loss
*   ((ΔP)/P1)heat addition loss = (T2/T1 -1)*((gamma M1^2)/2) * [1 + ((gamma -1)/2)*M2^2]^(gamma/(gamma-1) - 1/2)
*   ΔP/P = (gamma/2) * ((M1PRZ/CDgeometry) * (Acombustor/Aswirler))^2
*   Sprechamber = (sin theta/(1 + (tan theta)^-1)) * (Ap/Ae swirler)
*   Scombustor = Sprechamber * (d/D)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   gamma= Cp/Cv
*   M1PRZ = approach Mach number
*   CD geometry = geometric discharge coefficient
*   R: constant in equation
*   T2, T1: temperatures in equation
*   Mair
*   AE: effective area
*   Angle theta (θ): swirl angle (rad)
*   Ap: prechamber cross-sectional area (CSA) (mm²)
*   Ae swirler: swirler effective flow area (mm²)
*   d: prechamber i.d. (mm)
*   D: combustor i.d. (mm)
*   ΔP/P ≥ 4 percent (suggested for high combustion efficiency with radial inflow swirler)
*   ΔP/P ≥ 3.0 per cent (minimum condition for a perfectly stirred reactor)
*   Mach number < 0.05 (approaching the flame front)
*  Combustion efficiency > 99.99 percent

title: design_methods_of_the_Alstom_DLE_combustors
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 9]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Vmax = f(ΔPoverall) = Vref / (1 - B/100)
*   Vflashback = C(φoperating - φWE)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **(a) Variable Mixing Concept**
    *   **Axes:**
        *   Y axis: Mixing Parameter (s)
        *   X axis: Streamwise Distance from Swirler Leading Edge
    *   **Curves:** Decreasing curve from Radial Swirler to Shear Layer
*   **(b) NO Generation Hypothetical Mixing Parameter Time**
    *   **Axes:**
        *   Y axis: NO, ppm
        *   X axis: Residence Time
    *   **Curves:**
        *   Curve 1:  s = 0 line reaching equilibrium
        *   Curve 2: s > 0 rising above equilibrium, then falling to 2ms!

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **(a) Variable Mixing Concept**. Components in order are Radial Swirler leading to Prechamber then to Shear Layer which represents distances in flow
*   **(b) NO Generation Hypothetical Mixing parameter time** Stages in flow are Pre-combustion, Equillibrium and Post combustion

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Vref: Reference Velocity
*   Vmax: Maximum Axial Velocity
*   B: Blockage (inlet air flow blockage by the stabilizer)
*   C: Constant = 123
*   φWE: Equivalence ratio at weak extinction (for fully premixed ~ 0.41)
*   Inlet pressure (premixed stream): 0.56 Pa
*   Inlet Temperature: 610 to 700 K
*   60 Percent: Level achieved in radial swirler for partial premix
*   Between 15 and 20 percent mixing length in prechamber to achieve.

title: design_methods_of_the_Alstom_DLE_combustors
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 10]
Here's the technical information extracted from the text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Ymax = CCf * sqrt( (pf*Vf^2) / (pa*Va^2) )

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Axes:**
    *   x-axis: Power Output (%)
    *   y-axis: Maximum Fuel Jet Penetration (%)
*   **Curves:**
    *   A curve showing the relationship between Power Output and Maximum Fuel Jet Penetration (labeled "Approach Air Flow"). The curve is not given specific values.
*   **Key Values:**
    * Illustration of Fuel Jet Penetration at minimum Engine Load
    * Illustration of Fuel Jet Penetration at Full Engine Load

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   2 ms: Time to establish full premixing between air and fuel
*   S = 0: Mixing parameter for full premixing between air and fuel- NO approaches that of the equilibrium level
*   S>0: At that time the mixing parameter will overshoot with a spike of NO.

*   C: Constant ranging between 1.05 Cf <= C <= 1.25Cf
*   Cf: Function of fuel property and injector geometry
*   pf*Vf^2 / (pa*Va^2): Fuel-air jet momentum flux ratio

title: design_methods_of_the_Alstom_DLE_combustors
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 11]
Here's the extracted technical content:

**1. Mathematical equations:**

*   f(phi) = (1 / (sigma * sqrt(2*pi)) ) * exp( -[(phi - phi_bar)^2 / (2*sigma^2)] )
*   s = sigma / phi_bar
*   NOx = c1 * exp[c2 * phi_PZR * (1 - s) + c3 * s]
*   NOx = P^n_Tmean * f(Pilot %) * exp(cTflame)
*   0.13 <= n <= 0.35

**2. Graphs/charts:**

*   Figure 6: Mixing parameter effects on NOx production for various primary zone equivalence ratios.
    *   X axis: Primary Zone EQR (phi_PRZ)
        *   Values: 0.4 to 0.6
    *   Y axis: NOx (Corrected @15% O2), ppm
        *   Values: 0 to 100
    *   Curves:
        *   S = 0.1
        *   S = 0.15
        *   S = 0.2
        *   S = 0.25
        *   S = 0.3
    *   Arrow: increase in unmixedness

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:**

*   c1, c2, c3 are constants experimentally determined with typical values: 2*10^-5, 19.735, and 30, respectively.
*  s = 0 corresponds to perfect mixing
*   Commonly employed values of a radial swirler are 0.5 - 0.65.
*   phi: equivalence ratio
*   phi_bar: mean equivalence ratio
*   sigma: standard deviation
*   s: Mixing parameter
*   phi_PRZ: Mean primary reaction zone equivalence ratio
*   Pilot %: Percentage of pilot fuel
*   Tflame: Flame temperature
*   NOx: Nitrogen Oxide emissions

Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and diagram:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Wall temperature = f( TprZ, mCooling air, impingement pressure drop and film cooling effectiveness )
*   x/D= c * (delta P/P)^c1 * (1/T2)^c2
*   Nu = f(Rejet, (x/D), (y/D),(z/D), (Gc/Gj))

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 12: Schematic representation of the current DLE impingement hole arrangements**

    *   Components: Impingement air, Jet, Single Coolant Jet, Cross Flow, Combustor Wall Heat Conduction, TBC Layer, Combustion Heat Input, Radiation & Convection Heat Transfer
    *   Labels: m, v, R<sub>e</sub>, X, Z, Staggered Arrangement, X,Y

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, parameters:**

*   c, c1, c2 (constants in the x/D equation)
*   TPRZ (Temperature related pressure zone)
*   Mcooling air (Cooling air mass flow)
*   Rejet (Jet Reynolds number)
*   x/D, y/D and z/D  (Impingement array geometry ratios)
*   Gc/Gj (Ratio related to crossflow and jet mass velocity )
*   G60 (Early DLN combustion system name)
